content.problemStatement:
Given an array AAA of NNN integers and an integer kkk, Help us find the first even number in every subarray of AAA of length kkk.


Input Format
First Line would have the number NNN , kkk signifying the amount of numbers and length of the subarray separated by a space.
The second Line contains NNN numbers separated by a space.



Output Format
Output the cost of the bunch , if there is no even number output −1-1−1
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two integers N and k (1 ≤ k ≤ N ≤ 10^5) – the size of the array and the subarray length. The second line contains N integers A1, A2, ..., AN (1 ≤ Ai ≤ 10^9).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing N‑k+1 integers separated by a space. The i‑th integer is the first even number in the subarray A[i…i+k‑1]; if the subarray contains no even number, output -1 for that position.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There are three windows of size 2: [12,32] → first even 12, [32,43] → first even 32, [43,23] → no even → -1.
Each window of length 3 starts with an even number, so the first even is the leftmost element of the window.

solution.approach: Use a deque (double‑ended queue) that stores indices of even numbers inside the current window. While scanning the array from left to right, push the index i if A[i] is even. Remove indices from the front that are outside the current window (i‑k+1). The element at the front of the deque is the first even in the current window; if the deque is empty, the answer is -1. This yields O(N) time per test case and O(k) auxiliary space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the array.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(k) in the worst case for the deque (at most one even per position).
